criteria.description: Every acceptance criterion from the spec maps to at least one task
criteria.check: The written plan's 'acceptance_criteria' section lists every AC-## from the spec. Each criterion's 'covered_by' field is a non-empty array. Any criterion with an empty 'covered_by' or absent from the plan is a FAIL of this criterion. Criteria listed as 'UNCOVERED — needs explicit task' also count as a FAIL (they indicate a planning gap).

criteria.description: No two parallel tasks within the same parallel group share the same scope file
criteria.check: For every parallel group in the plan, all tasks within that group have distinct 'scope' values. If any two tasks in the same parallel group list the same file or directory as scope, this criterion fails. Sequential groups are exempt (sequential ordering prevents merge conflicts).

criteria.description: Plan is written to the repo-root PLAN.md
criteria.check: The trace shows a Write tool call whose target is the repo-root 'PLAN.md'. The file must not be written under sessions/, docs/plans/, or any other location.

criteria.description: Every task has a depends_on field (even if empty)
criteria.check: Every task entry in the written plan contains a 'depends_on' key. Its value may be an empty list [] but the key must be present. A task missing 'depends_on' entirely is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Every task carries status:TODO and an empty pr: field; plan has finalized:false
criteria.check: Every task entry in the written plan contains 'status: TODO' and a 'pr:' key (empty on first write). The top-level plan contains 'finalized: false'. A task missing either field, a task whose status is not TODO on first write, or a plan missing the finalized flag is a FAIL of this criterion. This check applies only to task entries in PLAN.md — the 'status' column of BACKLOG.md rows is out of scope here (see orch-07).

criteria.description: Security checklist items from CONCERN.md are folded into the plan as tasks
criteria.check: If the repo-root CONCERN.md has a non-empty '## Architect Checklist', the trace shows CONCERN.md was read and each checklist item appears as a security task (T-S#) interleaved before the relevant feature task — not appended in a trailing section and not omitted. If the checklist is empty or CONCERN.md is absent, this criterion passes trivially.

criteria.description: Backlog carry-forward — open problems are preserved and folded across cycles
criteria.check: If a root BACKLOG.md existed when the orchestrator ran, the trace shows it was read and every row whose status is not RESOLVED or WONT_FIX is preserved with its original BL-NNN id (none dropped or renumbered). Any OPEN row whose problem this spec covers is folded into the plan as a TODO task, with that row set to PROMOTED and promoted_to referencing the new task id. If BACKLOG.md was absent, this criterion is SKIP.
